title: PfaffianApplication
summary: Packages the Pfaffian-like specialization and formal Theorem 1.1 assembly.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Packages the Pfaffian-like specialization and formal Theorem 1.1 assembly.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Ambient/PE/Applications/PfaffianApplication.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Theorem 1.4 specialized to Pf_ell(W). In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,X\colon \operatorname{PESpace}.\operatorname{}{u, v},\;\operatorname{InvariantEvaluationModel},\;\operatorname{EinsteinAmbientLaplacianFormula},\;\operatorname{CaseEinsteinEvaluationFormula},\;\operatorname{PfLikeStraightness},\;P\colon \operatorname{PERenormalizationPackage} X,\;ell\colon \mathbb{N},\;hn\colon \operatorname{Even} X.\operatorname{bulkDim},\;hellPos\colon 1 <= \operatorname{ell},\;hellRange\colon \operatorname{ell} <= X.\operatorname{bulkDim} / 2,\quad \operatorname{RenormalizedCurvatureIntegral} (P.\operatorname{cutoffData} (\operatorname{WeylPfLike} \operatorname{ell})) = \operatorname{CaseCoeff} X.\operatorname{bulkDim} \operatorname{ell} \cdot P.\operatorname{convergentIntegral} (\operatorname{P\_l\_n} \operatorname{ell} X.\operatorname{bulkDim})
correspondence.leanType: theorem renormalizedIntegral_WeylPfLike (X : PESpace.{u, v}) [InvariantEvaluationModel] [EinsteinAmbientLaplacianFormula] [CaseEinsteinEvaluationFormula] [PfLikeStraightness] [P : PERenormalizationPackage X] (ell : Nat) (hn : Even X.bulkDim) (hellPos : 1 <= ell) (hellRange : ell <= X.bulkDim / 2) : RenormalizedCurvatureIntegral (P.cutoffData (WeylPfLike ell)) = CaseCoeff X.bulkDim ell * P.convergentIntegral (P_l_n ell X.bulkDim)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Coefficient of the P_(ell,n) term in Theorem 1.1. In explicit terms, for n, ell in the natural numbers, its defining expression is Nat.factorial ell minus 1 : Real divided by -2 to the power n divided by 2 minus ell times Nat.factorial n divided by 2 minus 1 : Real.

Coefficient of the renormalized volume term in Theorem 1.1. In explicit terms, for n in the natural numbers, its defining expression is -1 to the power n divided by 2 times double Factorial n minus 1 : Real.

The two external inputs for the Pfaffian application: Albin's renormalized Gauss--Bonnet identity; the Einstein Pfaffian reduction obtained by applying Theorem 1.4 termwise. Its mathematical data are: volume Data: PEVolume Cutoff Data X; volume normal Form: volume Data.normal Form equals P.normal Form; euler Characteristic: Int; albin Renormalized Pfaffian: P.RInt Full Pfaffian Invariant X.bulk Dim equals 2 times Real.pi to the power X.bulk Dim divided by 2 times euler Characteristic; pfaffian Reduction: P.RInt Full Pfaffian Invariant X.bulk Dim equals Pfaffian Volume Coeff X.bulk Dim times Renormalized Volume volume Data plus ∑ ell belongs to Finset.Icc 2 X.bulk Dim divided by 2, Pfaffian Case Coeff X.bulk Dim ell times P.convergent Integral P l n ell X.bulk Dim.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \begin{aligned}\operatorname{volumeData}&\colon \operatorname{PEVolumeCutoffData} X\\\operatorname{volume\_normalForm}&\colon \operatorname{volumeData}.\operatorname{normalForm} = P.\operatorname{normalForm}\\\operatorname{eulerCharacteristic}&\colon \mathbb{Z}\\\operatorname{albinRenormalizedPfaffian}&\colon P.\operatorname{RInt} (\operatorname{FullPfaffianInvariant} X.\operatorname{bulkDim}) = (2 \cdot \mathbb{R}.\operatorname{pi}) ^ (X.\operatorname{bulkDim} / 2) \cdot \operatorname{eulerCharacteristic}\\\operatorname{pfaffianReduction}&\colon P.\operatorname{RInt} (\operatorname{FullPfaffianInvariant} X.\operatorname{bulkDim}) = \operatorname{PfaffianVolumeCoeff} X.\operatorname{bulkDim} \cdot \operatorname{RenormalizedVolume} \operatorname{volumeData} + \sum \operatorname{ell} \in \operatorname{Finset}.\operatorname{Icc} 2 (X.\operatorname{bulkDim} / 2), \operatorname{PfaffianCaseCoeff} X.\operatorname{bulkDim} \operatorname{ell} \cdot P.\operatorname{convergentIntegral} (\operatorname{P\_l\_n} \operatorname{ell} X.\operatorname{bulkDim})\end{aligned}
correspondence.leanType: structure PfaffianApplicationData (X : PESpace.{u, v}) [InvariantEvaluationModel] [P : PERenormalizationPackage X]

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal Theorem 1.1. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,X\colon \operatorname{PESpace}.\operatorname{}{u, v},\;\operatorname{InvariantEvaluationModel},\;P\colon \operatorname{PERenormalizationPackage} X,\;D\colon \operatorname{PfaffianApplicationData} X,\quad (2 \cdot \mathbb{R}.\operatorname{pi}) ^ (X.\operatorname{bulkDim} / 2) \cdot D.\operatorname{eulerCharacteristic} = \operatorname{PfaffianVolumeCoeff} X.\operatorname{bulkDim} \cdot \operatorname{RenormalizedVolume} D.\operatorname{volumeData} + \sum \operatorname{ell} \in \operatorname{Finset}.\operatorname{Icc} 2 (X.\operatorname{bulkDim} / 2), \operatorname{PfaffianCaseCoeff} X.\operatorname{bulkDim} \operatorname{ell} \cdot P.\operatorname{convergentIntegral} (\operatorname{P\_l\_n} \operatorname{ell} X.\operatorname{bulkDim})
correspondence.leanType: theorem theorem_1_1_formal (X : PESpace.{u, v}) [InvariantEvaluationModel] [P : PERenormalizationPackage X] (D : PfaffianApplicationData X) : (2 * Real.pi) ^ (X.bulkDim / 2) * (D.eulerCharacteristic : Real) = PfaffianVolumeCoeff X.bulkDim * RenormalizedVolume D.volumeData + ∑ ell ∈ Finset.Icc 2 (X.bulkDim / 2), PfaffianCaseCoeff X.bulkDim ell * P.convergentIntegral (P_l_n ell X.bulkDim)

references.locator: Proposition 3.7, Proposition 3.10, Lemma 4.1, and Theorems 1.1/1.4 as applicable
references.note: Primary source for the renormalized-curvature-integral mechanism represented by these modules.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
